name: Dalton Schultz
headline: Volume king for Houston, but the touchdowns stay stubbornly scarce
body:
Schultz quietly turned in his best advanced profile yet in 2025, ranking sixth among tight ends with 0.355 EPA per play and a 58.5% success rate while soaking up a 19.2% target share. He ran 512 routes, drew 106 targets, and converted 82 of them into 777 yards on a tidy 77.4% catch rate with just two drops. That target volume and 0.207 targets per route run make him a dependable PPR floor and a genuine safety valve for CJ Stroud.

The ceiling is the problem. Despite 11 red-zone and six end-zone looks, he found the end zone only three times, extending a years-long touchdown drought that caps his scoring. With Nico Collins, Tank Dell, and the rookie pass-catchers competing for work, his efficiency stays modest. As a back-end TE2 going around the 11th round, he is a steady plug-in whose top-10 upside hinges entirely on touchdowns finally arriving.
